servinfosub.title: CVT Mechanical Description
servinfosub.ptxt: CVT can vary the actual gear ratio continuously and stepless from low-speed to high-speed range depending on the driving conditions. The CVT allows an improved driveability with reduced shift-shock in comparison with an existing A/T.
This full-automatic electrically-controlled transaxle offers forward gear, combining a continuously speed transmission design with a two-stage planetary gear unit, and 1-speed reverse gear.
By employing planetary gear unit, the gear ratio ranges have been expanded. Therefore, both good response during acceleration and silence when running at high speed have been improved.
The torque converter is of a 3-element, 1-step and 2-phase type and is equipped with an automatically controlled lock-up mechanism.
The gear change device consists of a planetary gear unit, multiple disc type clutch, multiple disc type brake, steel belt, primary pulley and secondary pulley.

The hydraulic pressure control device consists of a valve body assembly, line pressure control solenoid valve (linear solenoid), primary pressure control solenoid valve (linear solenoid), TCC solenoid valve (linear solenoid), low brake control solenoid valve (linear solenoid) and high clutch & reverse brake control solenoid valve (linear solenoid). Optimum line pressure complying with engine torque is produced by the pressure control solenoid valves in dependence upon control signal from TCM. This makes it possible to control the line pressure with high accuracy in accordance with the engine power and running conditions to achieve smooth shifting characteristics and high efficiency in power transmission.

The TCM can change the distance between the two pulley surfaces. To continuously vary its gear ratio, the TCM simultaneously adjusts the diameter of the primary and secondary pulleys. Engine torque is transmitted from secondary pulley to the wheels through the planetary gear unit. With continuously shifting of gear ratio, the CVT can avoid the shift-shock and deliver smooth driving.

entry.ptxt: Forward continuously variable + 2-step planetary gear type, reverse 1-step planetary gear type
Gear in neutral, output shaft fixed, engine start
Pulley ratio fixed + planetary gear in 1st
Forward continuously variable gear ratio + 2-step planetary gear ratio
Force feed system by oil pump
SUZUKI CVT FLUID GREEN 1 or Shell GREEN-1 V
Engages / releases the clutch or brake, altering the driving force transmitted to the reduction gear unit or the direction of rotation, according to clutch and brake operation.
Transmits driving force from counter driven gear shaft to secondary pulley.
Transmits driving force from primary pulley to reduction gear unit.
Transmits driving force from primary pulley to secondary pulley.

servinfosub.title: Operation Table of Mechanical Components
servinfosub.ptxt: : Engage
×: Disengage
: Controlled
—: Not applicable

servinfosub.title: CVT Control Description
servinfosub.ptxt: In order to achieve the optimum gear ratio for the driving force in accordance with the vehicle operating conditions, TCM detects vehicle conditions based on vehicle speed or input signals such as an accelerator pedal position.
TCM selects the optimum gear ratio and determines the gear change steps based on each input signal.
TCM controls the primary pressure to the primary pulley, decides the adjustable pulley position of the primary pulley, and controls the gear ratio.

By engaging the lock-up clutch in the torque convertor, shifting is controlled to eliminate slippage of the convertor and increase transmission efficiency. In addition, slip control at lock up clutch provides comfortable driving.

When the select lever is shifted to M range, or when the shift paddle switch is operated with the select lever in D range, TCM switches the control mode from automatic mode to manual mode, while maintaining the actual gear ratio.

In manual mode, every time the paddle-up switch or paddle-down switch is turned on, TCM selects the predetermined gear ratio for 1-speed to 7-speed, and performs shift up or shift down depending on the vehicle speed.
TCM distinguishes uphill or downhill according to vehicle speed, accelerator pedal position and/or brake pedal position, and controls gear ratio as follows:

item.ptxt: Uphill: To improve acceleration, gear ratio in the high speed range is limited.
Downhill: To obtain effective engine brakes, gear ratio is downshifted.

servinfosub.ptxt: If the select lever is shifted from D to R range while the vehicle is running at a specified speed or higher, TCM controls the transaxle operation so that it does not shift into the reverse gear, in order to protect CVT inside parts from damage.

A neutral idle control that controls the planetary gear unit in neutral while the vehicle is stopping with D selected (and the brake pedal is depressed) is employed. Therefore, engine load is reduced, and fuel efficiency while the vehicle is stopped at a red light has been improved.

servinfosub.title: Operation Table of Solenoid Valve

entry.ptxt: High clutch & reverse brake control solenoid valve

servinfosub.ptxt: : High duty
: Middle duty
×: Low duty
*1: Also applies to neutral idle control.

entry.ptxt: High clutch & reverse brake control solenoid valve
